plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-550 RP17.P09.M06.S07-RP17.P09.M10.S10 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-550 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP17.P09 closeout handoff slice: the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set tail (the fourteen remaining rows), the full M07 Test And Golden Case Set cycle (twenty-two rows), the full M08 Hermes Evidence Packet cycle (twenty rows), the full M09 Claude Review Packet cycle (twenty rows), and the full M10 Closeout And Next Handoff cycle (ten rows) as five descriptor-only sections over 86 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H17), Claude review packet (C17), and CP00-551 handoff closing RP17 AI Governance and routing to RP18 AI Legal Workflows, without opening AI governance, model policy, retrieval scope, or citation runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-550 is a clean descriptor-only closeout pack (RP17.P09.M06.S07-RP17.P09.M10.S10, risk C, 86 units) that closes RP17 AI Governance and hands off to RP18.P00.M00.S01. Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level production_ready claim is FALSE and honestly supported: implementation_layer=descriptor, runtime_ready=false, and no overclaim of runtime/enterprise trust. Pack identity (id, risk, unit_count, range, next pack/subphase) is consistent across review-baseline, registry.js, the contract JSON, the manifest, the validate script, the test file, and the live closeout-pack-plan.json. The deliverable distribution (implementation 57, claude_review 12, security_audit 8, test 4, ui 4, hermes_evidence 1) and per-micro-phase counts (M06 14, M07 22, M08 20, M09 20, M10 10) were manually recounted from the plan units and both sum to 86. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: no fs/network/child_process/process.env usage exists anywhere in packages/ai-governance/src, and the resolved cp550_no_write_attestation has every runtime/write/leak flag false. Authority boundary is intact: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, human final approval required, and Claude tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob. Hermes is used only for the deterministic descriptor review packet/receipt (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; receipt_shape descriptor_only...no_write), not product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is satisfied: forbidden_review_evidence enumerates auth-failure, zero-byte, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing-field cases; the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON; tests and the validate script assert against live plan data via deepEqual; and command-evidence records all implementation/plan/repo gates at exit 0. Tests and validators are meaningful (coverage validated against the live plan, descriptor rows asserted descriptor-only, authority boundaries asserted). The dirty files scripts/progress-control-room-data.mjs and tools/progress-control-room/index.html are correctly recorded as unrelated (UI workstream) and are not part of this pack scope. No P0-P3 findings. Does not block pack closeout or goal closeout. Note: Claude is a read-only reviewer, not the final approver; human final approval and the enterprise trust claim are closed outside this receipt.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-550 ai governance core p09 closeout handoff slice descriptor pack
